title: OLAP Warehouse: Snowflake (Cloud-Agnostic)
description: Columnar data warehouse on Snowflake, deployable on AWS, Azure, or GCP. Snowflake's unique architecture separates storage (S3/ADLS/GCS), compute (virtual warehouses), and query coordination (cloud services layer) independently. Multiple virtual warehouses can run concurrently against the same data without contention, enabling near-linear concurrency scaling. Best for teams requiring cloud portability, multi-cloud access to the same data, or high analytical concurrency beyond what single-cloud managed warehouses support. Query latency is 200ms-10s; simple cached queries return in under 200ms with result cache. Multi-cluster warehouses scale concurrency to 1,000+ simultaneous queries.

cost.costNotes: Credits billed per virtual warehouse second. X-Small warehouse: $2/credit (~$0.0006/sec). X-Large: $16/credit. Storage: $23/TB/mo compressed. Free trial: $400 credits.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Min ($50): X-Small warehouse running 2 hours/day for development ($2/credit × 0.5 credits/hr × 60hr/mo = $60). Max ($20,000): multiple X-Large multi-cluster warehouses for high-concurrency production ($16/credit × 8 credits/hr × 720hr/mo × 2 clusters = $18,400) + storage. Most production teams land $500-$5,000/mo with auto-suspend and right-sizing.
provenance.adoptionCost: Snowflake is operationally simpler than Redshift/Synapse. Adoption cost is dbt pipeline setup, Fivetran/Airbyte connectors, and BI tool integration. Estimate 12-15 developer days at ~$160/day = $2,500.
provenance.licenseCost: No traditional software license. Snowflake charges on credit consumption. Business Critical and VPS editions have higher per-credit pricing for advanced security features.

provides.reasoning: Snowflake delivers columnar MPP query execution with independent compute scaling via virtual warehouses.
Integrates with Tableau, Power BI, Looker, Sigma, and any JDBC/ODBC-compatible BI tool. Native Snowsight UI for ad-hoc queries.
dbt-snowflake is the dominant ELT tool. Snowpipe for continuous ingestion. Fivetran, Airbyte, and Stitch have native Snowflake connectors.

requires.reasoning: Snowflake expects data via COPY INTO from cloud storage, Snowpipe streaming, or Kafka Snowflake Connector. Direct high-frequency row inserts are possible but expensive.
Snowflake uses the underlying cloud's object storage (S3/ADLS/GCS) for its internal storage layer. External tables can query cloud storage directly.

availability.reason: Cloud-agnostic pattern applicable to AWS, Azure, and GCP deployments

supports_nfr.reason: Snowflake p95 is 200ms-10s for analytical queries. Result cache serves repeated queries in under 100ms; local disk cache warms after first execution. Cold queries on large datasets take 2-30s.
Snowflake SLA is 99.9% for Standard, 99.99% for Business Critical edition. Multi-region failover available on Business Critical with Business Continuity add-on.
Snowflake virtual warehouses start in seconds. RTO is effectively 1-5 minutes for cloud-level zone issues; platform-level failures are rare with 99.9-99.99% SLA.
Snowflake Time Travel provides point-in-time recovery up to 90 days (Enterprise edition). Fail-safe provides an additional 7-day non-configurable recovery window.

Multi-cluster Snowflake warehouses scale to ~1,000 concurrent queries. Above this, query routing logic and cloud services layer become bottlenecks. Most analytical workloads do not approach this limit.
Snowflake COPY INTO and Snowpipe support high-throughput batch ingestion. Above ~100 write QPS, Kafka Snowflake Connector or Snowpipe Streaming is required.
Snowflake supports username/password, OAuth 2.0, key-pair authentication, MFA, and SAML SSO via Okta, Azure AD, and other IdPs.
Snowflake ACCOUNT_USAGE schema provides full query history, login history, and access events. Compatible with enterprise audit requirements.
Snowflake supports dynamic data masking, row access policies, column-level security, and end-to-end encryption. Compatible with PII workloads.
Compatible with GDPR when data residency controls, column masking, and right-to-erasure pipeline procedures are in place.
Compatible with HIPAA when warehouse-level encryption and appropriate BAA coverage are configured.
Compatible with CCPA when consumer data deletion triggers a pipeline reprocess that excludes deleted records.
Compatible with SOX when query audit logs provide an immutable trail of data access and transformation operations.
Durability is handled by the underlying object storage layer (S3, GCS, ADLS). All major managed warehouses provide durable storage by default.

supports_constraints.reason: Snowflake is a cross-cloud SaaS product. This variant activates only when snowflake is explicitly opted-in via constraints.saas-providers, distinguishing it from the cloud-native OLAP variants (--aws, --gcp, --azure).
Snowflake deploys on AWS, Azure, and GCP. Cloud-agnostic choice for teams avoiding cloud vendor lock-in.
Snowflake provides JDBC/ODBC drivers and is agnostic to language.
Snowflake serves as analytical backend for any platform.
Snowflake is selected specifically for OLAP analytical workloads.

edition.description: standard: core warehouse features. enterprise: multi-cluster warehouses, column-level security, Time Travel up to 90 days. business-critical: HIPAA/PCI compliance, private connectivity, failover.

warehouse_size.description: Controls compute nodes per cluster. Doubles in cost and parallelism at each step. Medium is a common production starting point. Scale up for faster complex queries; scale down with auto-suspend for cost savings.

multi_cluster.description: Automatically adds warehouse clusters during concurrency spikes. Requires Enterprise edition. Recommended when peak analytical QPS exceeds ~50 concurrent queries.

auto_suspend_seconds.description: Suspend warehouse after N seconds of inactivity to reduce cost. 300s (5 min) is a common production setting. Set lower for dev/test; disable for always-on production warehouses.

ingestion_mode.description: copy-into: bulk COPY from S3/ADLS/GCS (batch ETL). snowpipe: micro-batch continuous ingestion (~60s lag). kafka-connector: Kafka to Snowflake streaming. snowpipe-streaming: row-level streaming API for sub-second ingestion.

query_result_cache.description: Snowflake result cache serves identical queries from cache for 24 hours at no compute cost. Always enabled by default; disable only if stale results are unacceptable.
